feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: The Best After-Dinner Drink for Better Blood Sugar, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Discover why unsweetened decaf green tea is the best after-dinner drink for blood sugar, with dietitian-backed tips for supporting healthy glucose levels.

entry.title: East End salmonella outbreak being investigated by Suffolk County health officials
entry.summary: Dozens of people have been hospitalized with the infection since the beginning of July.

entry.title: Woman Gets Life-Changing Breast Reduction From Size ’36G to 36C’
entry.summary: Ellen spent years convincing herself that she did not need the procedure. Now, she’s overjoyed that she finally did.

entry.title: 5 signs you’re aging well — and the red flags to watch for in your 40s and 50s
entry.summary: While tech entrepreneurs and biohackers race to rewrite the rules of longevity, there are everyday telltale signs you’re aging well without a health tracker or blood tests.

entry.title: How to walk to reap the cardio benefits, according to experts
entry.summary: Here’s how to think through your walking habit, and how it fits into your overall exercise routine.

entry.title: The latest science on cannabis has some good news and bad news
entry.summary: Recent studies have shed light on the effect of cannabis consumption.

entry.title: Irritable patients’ impact on emergency department doctors uncovered by UMass Amherst study
entry.summary: A study led by UMass Amherst found that emergency department physicians were more likely to become disengaged in patient care when dealing with irritable patients, potentially affecting the quality of care they provide.

entry.title: Colorado’s health insurance marketplace loses one insurer, gains another
entry.summary: Colorado’s individual health insurance marketplace will gain its first new option in three years in 2027, while losing another.

entry.title: The 7 Best Foods to Eat to Support Bone Density Midlife, According to a Doctor
entry.summary: In TODAY.com’s Expert Tip of the Day, a doctor and fitness expert shares how to incorporate nutrients to prevent bone loss in midlife.

entry.title: 5 Easy Ways to Get 10 Grams of Fiber at Breakfast
entry.summary: Try one of these easy breakfast options recommended by dietitians to get at least 10 grams of fiber first thing in the morning.

entry.title: Oncologist Reveals the One Condiment Linked to Increased Cancer Risk
entry.summary: It’s common at summer barbecues, but there’s a healthier alternative.

entry.title: U.S. Navy microbiologist discovers 14 new viruses and gives them Texas names
entry.summary: A U.S. Navy microbiologist found 14 new bacteria-eating viruses that could help fight drug-resistant infections and gave them Texas names.

entry.title: Influencers are hopping on a surprising wearable trend that requires a needle — do you need one?
entry.summary: Healthy influencers are embracing a wearable once reserved for diabetes patients to optimize their health. But experts say that the benefits for most people remain unproven.

entry.title: The 2 brain-healthy habits a neurologist swears by — that have nothing to do with supplements or puzzles
entry.summary: A neurologist shared why emotional health is so important for reducing the risk of dementia and Alzheimer's.

entry.title: Why Do Statins Hurt Muscles? Scientists May Finally Have an Answer
entry.summary: The discovery may explain why some people experience side effects from statins and could lead to future therapies that make the drugs easier to tolerate.

entry.title: Scientists develop a groundbreaking vaccine that outsmarts illicit fentanyl analogs
entry.summary: A novel vaccine candidate could offer broad protection against fentanyl and its many illicit variants. By training the immune system to recognize a shared molecular signature, the breakthrough approach outpaces the rapid development of synthetic designer opio…

entry.title: Why tick bites are surging in the US this year
entry.summary: Lone Star Tick-CDC ByAmelia TwymanWASHINGTON — The prime time for tick

entry.title: Chronic Constipation May Be Linked to Your Gut's 'Second Brain'
entry.summary: Enjoying consistent, low-effort bowel movements is a massively underappreciated aspect of one's quality of life.

entry.title: OB-GYN pay overhaul spells changes for maternal care
entry.summary: The U.S. has the highest maternal mortality rate among peer countries.

entry.title: 58 People Hospitalized From East End Salmonella Outbreak
entry.summary: The New York State Health Department has been notified about the public health investigation.

entry.title: Common knee surgery may do more harm than good: "I don't know how I would defend this procedure"
entry.summary: A Finnish study followed patients for 10 years after they had a popular knee surgery. For many, the pain continued or even worsened.

entry.title: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
entry.summary: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.

entry.title: 'Once it's gone, it's gone': How to future-proof your hearing
entry.summary: It's not just rocket launches and death metal concerts that lead to hearing loss. Many everyday activities can cause damage. Here's what you can do to protect your ears.

entry.title: A breakthrough in pancreatic cancer has experts excited — and braced for what’s to come
entry.summary: Pancreatic cancer specialists are excited about the biggest breakthrough in the field in decades. They are also looking around the corner to what comes next.

entry.title: “What Are You Doing?”: MIL Caught Doing The Thing She Was Supposed To Apologize For, Couple Go No-Contact
entry.summary: Allergies can cause serious pain and discomfort, even when they aren’t life-threatening. One woman repeatedly warned her mother-in-law that she was allergic to dairy and asked her not to include it in any meals. Yet despite several reminders and clear explana…

entry.title: A Human-Safe Drug Reduces Multiple Signs of Alzheimer's in Mice
entry.summary: Early in the progression of Alzheimer's and other neurodegenerative diseases, DNA inside neurons starts showing signs of weakness.

entry.title: Scientists discover a hidden heart valve risk linked to gum disease
entry.summary: Researchers found that bacteria linked to gum disease may help drive the development of calcific aortic valve stenosis by triggering inflammation and calcium buildup in the heart valve. The early findings suggest that keeping gums healthy could play a role in…

entry.title: Major international trial finds healthy behaviors help brain health in older adults
entry.summary: The question researchers still want to answer is whether those changes translate to a reduced risk of dementia.

entry.title: Her In-Laws Thought Weight Comments Were Acceptable, So She Set About Proving Them Wrong
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: What to know: Two cases of cyclosporiasis confirmed in NH, DHHS says
entry.summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is tracking cases of cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection, in 31 states — including New Hampshire. Here's what you need to know.

entry.title: U.S. citizen working for humanitarian organization tests positive for Ebola in DR Congo
entry.summary: The outbreak has caused at least 1,830 confirmed cases in Congo, including 648 deaths, health officials said. Cases have also been confirmed in neighboring Uganda.

entry.title: Low-protein ‘longevity diet’ helped mice live healthier, leaner lives: Study
entry.summary: Research from the University of Southern California found the "longevity diet" reduced body fat and lowered frailty in older mice.

entry.title: How Indian scientists are mapping the brain's last frontier
entry.summary: India's new brainstem atlas offers scientists an unprecedented map of one of the brain's least known regions.

entry.title: Doctors, Dentists, And Other Medical Pros Are Sharing Things Patients Often Overlook But Shouldn't
entry.summary: "Don't brush it off."

entry.title: NH DHHS: Two cases of cyclosporiasis confirmed in the state in 2026
entry.summary: It's an intestinal illness that can come from unwashed produce

entry.title: Researchers Identify Age of Fertility Wall for Women
entry.summary: Even IVF with donor eggs can't fully offset age-related decline after 49, study finds

entry.title: Exclusive | Extremely rare cancer striking kids in rich SoCal city takes worrying turn — as possible cause emerges
entry.summary: In a statement to The California Post, the Ladera Ranch Maintenance Corporation (LARMAC), which oversees landscaping throughout the community, expressed sympathy for affected families but said ther…

entry.title: It’s not just Depo-Provera — study links 5 more birth control drugs to brain tumor risk
entry.summary: Women who use Depo-Provera or other hormonal birth control pills face a higher risk of developing a slow-growing rare brain tumor, known as a meningioma.

entry.title: Creatine May Do More Than Boost Muscle Performance
entry.summary: Scientists say supplement often used by gym rats may also help fight cancer

entry.title: Second measles case recorded in Tennessee
entry.summary: The Tennessee Department of Health on Saturday confirmed a second case of measles in Middle Tennessee.

entry.title: ‘Pain like I’ve never had’: women on the trauma of womb procedure
entry.summary: Over 70,000 hysteroscopies are performed each year but hospitals do not routinely offer sedation or anaesthetic in what campaigners say is ‘medical misogyny’

entry.title: Medicine is Moving From Calendars to Clocks
entry.summary: Proteins Tell How Fast Our Organs and Cells Are Ticking

entry.title: Japanese Find Frog Bacteria That Wipes Out Cancer Tumors With a Single Dose
entry.summary: Japanese scientists found a natural gut bacteria in frogs with remarkable anti-cancer traits—in fact, it destroyed tumors in one dose.

entry.title: Salmonella Outbreak That Hospitalized 58 East End Residents Under Investigation
entry.summary: A total of 58 patients have been seen by Stony Brook Southampton Hospital doctors while exhibiting symptoms consistent with salmonella infection since the beginning of July, according to hospital officials.

entry.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
entry.summary: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.

entry.title: The Best Time of Day to Nap, According to Sleep Experts
entry.summary: Sleep experts recommend napping between 1 and 3 p.m. Learn why this is the best time to nap and how a short afternoon snooze may improve alertness, memory and recovery.

entry.title: Grand Teton National Park tourists might have been exposed to measles
entry.summary: Park shares details after second case of measles is confirmed in Teton County.

entry.title: Can fabric softener and dryer sheets give you cancer?
entry.summary: The science behind the viral claims that these staples expose people to chemicals linked to cancer is a bit more nuanced.

entry.title: Why everyone’s turning to minoxidil for hair growth
entry.summary: Discover how minoxidil works for hair growth, who it is recommended for, its benefits and potential side effects, and how to use it properly.

entry.title: Health officials warn of potential measles exposure at Philadelphia International Airport
entry.summary: The Health Department says a person with measles traveled through the airport on the Fourth of July.
